summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by announcing Impact Church's upcoming Resurrection Sunday service at the Mullet Arena and then preached from Mark 2:1-12, where four friends brought a paralyzed man to Jesus by digging through a roof. He titled the message 'Let's Rip the Roof Off,' challenging the congregation to break through the 'roofs' or limitations in their own lives—such as fear, shame, addiction, and doubt—that keep them from God's presence and purpose. The sermon centered on three prayers believers should pray daily as they approach Easter: for compassion, conviction, and courage to reach the lost. Pastor Travis emphasized that the world is spiritually paralyzed and numb, and that Christians are called to carry others to Jesus, pushing past the 'crowds' of distraction, complacency, and fear. He called for men to step up in biblical masculinity and for everyone to have faith not just for themselves but for others. The message concluded with a call to action: to invest financially in the Resurrection Sunday outreach and to actively invite friends, family, and coworkers, praying for them by name.
mainPoints: Pray for a God-given compassion to see and feel the spiritual paralysis of those who don't know Jesus.
Pray for a Holy Spirit-fueled conviction that pushes past the 'crowds' of life to bring people to Christ.
Pray for the courage and faith to act, inviting and carrying others into the presence of Jesus.

versesMentioned.text: A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home. They gathered in such large numbers that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them. Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them. Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.' Now some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves, 'Why does this fellow talk like that? He’s blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?' Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, 'Why are you thinking these things? Which is easier: to say to this paralyzed man, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat and walk’? But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.' So he said to the man, 'I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.' He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, 'We have never seen anything like this!'
But if I say, 'I will not mention his word or speak anymore in his name,' his word is in my heart like a fire, a fire shut up in my bones. I am weary of holding it in; indeed, I cannot.
Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.

Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share. In this way they will lay up treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the coming age, so that they may take hold of the life that is truly life.
Then the master told his servant, 'Go out to the roads and country lanes and compel them to come in, so that my house will be full.'

Monday.scripture: Mark 2:3-4 - 'Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them. Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof...'
Monday.action: Identify one person in your life who seems spiritually numb or distant from God. Write their name in your phone's notes app.
Monday.prayer: God, give me your eyes to see the spiritual paralysis around me. Stir in me a genuine compassion for [Name] today.

Tuesday.scripture: Jeremiah 20:9 - '...his word is in my heart like a fire, a fire shut up in my bones. I am weary of holding it in; indeed, I cannot.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on one 'crowd' (distraction, fear, convenience) that has kept you from sharing your faith. Decide on one step to push past it this week.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, ignite a holy conviction in me. Let your Word be a fire in my bones that compels me to act, regardless of the crowd.

Wednesday.scripture: Joshua 1:9 - 'Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.'
Wednesday.action: Pray for the person on your list by name. Then, send them a simple, kind text message to check in on them.
Wednesday.prayer: God, make me strong and courageous. Give me the faith to believe you can work through me to reach [Name]. I trust you are with me.

Thursday.scripture: Mark 2:5 - 'When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.''
Thursday.action: Think of a specific need your friend has (emotional, physical, spiritual). In your private prayer time, intercede for them with faith for their breakthrough.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I bring [Name] before you today. I ask that you would meet their deepest need and draw them to yourself, just as you saw the faith of the four friends.

Friday.scripture: 1 Timothy 6:18-19 - 'Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share...'
Friday.action: Prayerfully consider making a financial investment in your church's outreach or a ministry that shares the Gospel. If you already give, thank God for the privilege.
Friday.prayer: Father, show me how to store up treasure in heaven. Help me to be generous and willing to share, investing in what lasts for eternity.

Saturday.scripture: Luke 14:23 - '...compel them to come in, so that my house will be full.'
Saturday.action: Directly invite the person you've been praying for to join you for a church service or a Resurrection Sunday event. Make the ask simple and personal.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, you compel us to fill your house. Give me the right words and boldness to invite [Name] into your presence this weekend.
